title: Wrap from Content Constraints, Not Device Labels
summary: Build and verify flex wrapping in service card row at narrow and translated widths through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain flex wrapping from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply flex wrapping without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Service card row at narrow and translated widths case 1: flex-wrap alone guarantees every card has a usable minimum width.
Service card row at narrow and translated widths case 1: set a content-derived flexible basis and minimum, enable wrapping, and test when real cards move to the next line across content changes.
Service card row at narrow and translated widths case 1: set flex-wrap and width 10% on every card so at least ten always fit.

steps.title: 02 · Read the service card row at narrow and translated widths case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 2: Which claim preserves flex wrapping when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: service card row at narrow and translated widths.
steps.why: Wrap from Content Constraints, Not Device Labels makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 2 in service card row at narrow and translated widths.

content.text: Flex-wrap allows items to create additional flex lines, but usable wrapping also needs a meaningful flexible basis, shrink behavior, min size, gaps, and content resilience. Wrapping decisions should follow when content becomes cramped or overflows rather than a fixed device-specific item count. Each line distributes free space independently. Apply it to service card row at narrow and translated widths.

options.text: Service card row at narrow and translated widths case 2: flex-wrap alone guarantees every card has a usable minimum width.
Service card row at narrow and translated widths case 2: set a content-derived flexible basis and minimum, enable wrapping, and test when real cards move to the next line across content changes.
Service card row at narrow and translated widths case 2: set flex-wrap and width 10% on every card so at least ten always fit.
